task_completion.description: Open the correct Jitsi Meet configuration file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor (like nano, vim, gedit, or VS Code) or terminal window displaying the contents of the file located at '/home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/custom-config.js'.

task_completion.description: Modify the toolbarButtons array in the configuration file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'toolbarButtons' array in the configuration file being edited. It must be modified to contain exactly the 5 required items: 'microphone', 'camera', 'hangup', 'tileview', and 'fullscreen'.

task_completion.description: Open the Jitsi Meet web interface to verify changes
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a web browser displaying an active Jitsi Meet meeting room interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the required buttons are present in the meeting toolbar
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the toolbar at the bottom of the Jitsi Meet interface. It must visibly display the icons for microphone, camera, hangup (usually a red phone), tile view (four squares), and fullscreen.

task_completion.description: Verify no prohibited buttons are present in the meeting toolbar
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the toolbar at the bottom of the Jitsi Meet interface. It must ONLY contain the 5 required buttons. Ensure there are no icons for chat, screen share (desktop), raise hand, participants/invite, or other extra features.

integrity.description: Verify the agent modified the actual configuration file, not a copy
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the file path in the text editor or terminal title/header to ensure it is exactly '/home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/custom-config.js'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a real web browser to check the meeting interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the Jitsi Meet interface is loaded in a standard web browser (e.g., Chromium, Firefox) with a valid URL (typically localhost or a local IP), not a local HTML file or mock-up.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the toolbar appearance
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element) are not open and actively hiding toolbar elements in the DOM to fake the screenshot.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The configuration file to modify is exactly '/home/ga/.jitsi-meet-cfg/web/custom-config.js'. The 'toolbarButtons' array in this file must be set to include only: 'microphone', 'camera', 'hangup', 'tileview', and 'fullscreen'. In the Jitsi Meet browser interface, verify that ONLY these 5 buttons are visible on the bottom toolbar. Prohibited buttons that must NOT be visible include: chat, desktop (screen share), invite, raisehand, security, videoquality, filmstrip, shortcuts, stats, and feedback.
